data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-09T01:45:00.198Z</current_datetime>

I'm migrating a .NET 10 web API to .NET 11. The app uses EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider.
We have a repository pattern where some methods use synchronous LINQ:

```csharp
public class ProductRepository
{
    private readonly CosmosDbContext _context;

    public List<Product> GetAll()
    {
        return _context.Products.ToList();
    }

    public Product GetById(int id)
    {
        return _context.Products.First(p => p.Id == id);
    }

    public int GetCount()
    {
        return _context.Products.Count();
    }

    public void Save(Product product)
    {
        _context.Products.Add(product);
        _context.SaveChanges();
    }
}
```

In .NET 10 we had the sync-over-async opt-in configured. What breaks in .NET 11?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about what breaks when migrating from .NET 10 to .NET 11 with EF Core using the Azure Cosmos DB provider, specifically around synchronous LINQ operations.

This is a knowledge question about .NET/EF Core breaking changes. Let me answer from what I know:

In EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider, there was a significant change regarding synchronous operations. The Cosmos DB provider has always been inherently asynchronous (since the underlying Cosmos SDK is async-only). In earlier versions, EF Core allowed "sync-over-async" as an opt-in, where synchronous calls would internally block on async operations.


In .NET 11, the Cosmos provider removed support for synchronous operations entirely — calling sync methods now throws an `InvalidOperationException` at runtime with no way to opt back in. I'm trying to pin down exactly when this transition happened, but the key point is that by .NET 11, there's no longer any fallback mechanism for sync-over-async behavior on the Cosmos provider. Cosmos provider removes the sync-over-async opt-in entirely in .NET 11, so all synchronous LINQ operations like `ToList()`, `First()`, and `SaveChanges()` will throw `InvalidOperationException` at runtime instead of executing.
